case_name: Laredo Texas Hospital Company, L.P. D/B/A Laredo Medical Center v. Zulema Cabrera, Individually and on Behalf of the Estate of David Cabrera

summary.brief: Laredo Texas Hospital Company, L.P. D/B/A Laredo Medical Center v. Zulema Cabrera, Individually and on Behalf of the Estate of David Cabrera. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: On May 31, 2019, Zulema’s husband, David Cabrera, underwent a tonsillectomy at LMC.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: David remained in the ICU from the evening of Friday, May 31 until Tuesday, June 4.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: David’s hemoglobin levels, which indicate the health of red blood cells, dropped throughout his stay in the ICU.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Wittenberg testified that David’s falling hemoglobin levels were a sign of untreated internal bleeding.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Aaron Wittenberg

evidence_index.text: David was transferred from the ICU to LMC’s medical-surgical floor on June 4, five days after his surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On June 14, 2019, David coughed, ran to the bathroom, and called out for Zulema. When Zulema arrived in the bathroom, “the sink was full of blood.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: David’s death certificate identifies his cause of death as “exsanguination— or mass bleeding—following tonsillectomy . . . and that the interval that this had been occurring over was two weeks.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Wittenberg testified that he believed David died from a ruptured pseudoaneurysm in his throat that could have been prevented through examination and treatment by an interventional radiologist.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Aaron Wittenberg

evidence_index.text: A defense medical expert, Dr. Gilbert Ruiz, testified that he believed David died from a spontaneous delayed bleed, which he described as a known but unpredictable risk of a tonsillectomy.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Gilbert Ruiz

evidence_index.text: We affirm the trial court’s judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: David Cabrera was scheduled for a tonsillectomy at Laredo Medical Center.

requestables.name: CT Angiogram of Neck
requestables.description: CT angiogram to assess for vascular injury

reveal.result_summary: CT angiogram performed but findings not detailed in judgment
reveal.clinical_significance: Used to assess for vascular injury
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt further vascular intervention if abnormalities found

requestables.name: Hemoglobin Levels
requestables.description: Serial hemoglobin measurements to monitor for bleeding

reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt intervention to address bleeding

requestables.name: Interventional Radiology Consult
requestables.description: Consult with interventional radiology to assess and treat potential vascular injury

reveal.clinical_significance: Critical for identifying and treating vascular injury
reveal.decision_impact: Failure to complete consult contributed to adverse outcome

timeline_phases.name: Postoperative ICU Stay
timeline_phases.description: Patient monitored in ICU following surgery

timeline_phases.name: Discharge and Home Care
timeline_phases.description: Patient discharged home despite ongoing symptoms

key_events.event: Patient discharged with open interventional radiology order
Patient experiences severe bleeding at home

timeline_phases.name: ICU Monitoring
timeline_phases.description: Monitoring of patient in ICU with falling hemoglobin levels

timeline_phases.name: Transfer to Medical-Surgical Floor
timeline_phases.description: Patient transferred from ICU to medical-surgical floor

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ICU with falling hemoglobin levels
decision_points.prompt: What should be done in response to falling hemoglobin levels?

options.description: Initiate interventional radiology consult to address potential bleeding
options.clinical_reasoning: Necessary to identify and treat source of bleeding
Failed to address ongoing bleeding risk

actual_action_defendant.description: Patient was monitored without completing interventional radiology consult

expected_action_court.description: Interventional radiology consult should have been completed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated necessity for intervention

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to complete consult led to undiagnosed bleeding
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Interventional radiology should have been involved
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care required addressing ongoing bleeding

patient_outcome.description: Patient died from exsanguination following a ruptured pseudoaneurysm

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to investigate falling hemoglobin levels
Causation established - failure to investigate led to undiagnosed internal bleeding

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to investigate falling hemoglobin levels
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Investigation would have identified internal bleeding

factual_timeline.event: Patient transferred from ICU to medical-surgical floor
Patient discharged with open interventional radiology order
Patient experienced severe bleeding and died
Patient underwent tonsillectomy and was admitted to ICU
Patient transferred from ICU to medical-surgical floor

treatments.outcome: Patient remained stable but with falling hemoglobin

allegations.allegation: Failure to complete interventional radiology consult
Failure to investigate falling hemoglobin levels

court_findings.finding: LMC's negligence proximately caused the occurrence

court_findings.legal_significance: Failure to investigate falling hemoglobin levels
